description: §41.15.7 (a) compile-time rejection — the User struct has a struct-typed field (address: Address). v1.0 has no foreign-key derivation rule for cross-type struct references (OQ-SCH-4 ratified out-of-scope), so the type-system stage rejects the nested struct field with E-SCHEMAFOR-NESTED-STRUCT-NO-FK-V1 rather than silently guessing an FK column.
rationale: §41.15.7 normative statement: a struct field whose declared type is itself a :struct SHALL emit E-SCHEMAFOR-NESTED-STRUCT-NO-FK-V1 at the type-system stage (§34); auto-derived foreign-key columns for cross-type struct references are RESERVED for v1.next. Resolution: omit the nested struct field via schemaFor(T, { omit: [...] }) and hand-author the FK column inside the same <schema> block per §41.15.3 interleaving, OR refactor to a flat _id field.
